page: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 128.0 GB of system memory, which provides a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 1229 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place the D32ds_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. The single-core stress-ng score is 1900.09, while the multi-core performance reaches 31064.56. Memory bandwidth tests show 1679.52 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 146.94 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The system demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small 135M LLM models at 287.54 tokens/sec, though performance drops significantly on larger models, such as 2.29 tokens/sec for a 70B model. Database operations score 14911.05 on Passmark, and Redis SET operations achieve 31.36 million operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the D32ds_v5 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. The inclusion of a 1229 GB local disk provides storage capacity without requiring external attachments, enhancing cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and solid multi-threaded decompression capabilities of 152.59 GB/sec on bzip3, this server is well-suited for relational databases, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. It features a bundled 1229 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers balanced performance across standard benchmarks, including a Passmark CPU score of 36630.16 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.36 million operations per second. While it exhibits strong text generation speeds of 287.54 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, performance degrades on larger 70B models. This server is optimized for general-purpose workloads, enterprise web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk, delivering balanced performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial memory, and high-capacity local storage for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM and a bundled 1229 GB local storage disk.
Delivers 31.36 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks.
Achieves strong text generation speeds of 287.54 tokens/sec for small LLM models.
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
